summary: Senior Software Engineer with 12+ years building production web and mobile systems with React, TypeScript, Next.js, React Native, REST APIs, and WebSockets. Strong match for payments and blockchain-adjacent work: built Stripe and Transbank payment flows, co-led OpenZeppelin Defender Secure Audit for smart contract auditors, and owned EVM Relayer reliability work covering RPC retry and failover, cloud KMS signers, transaction cost handling, and a critical nonce race fix. Brings senior IC depth in frontend architecture, secure financial software, testing, and delivery systems, with results such as cutting TypeScript monorepo builds from about 15 minutes to 3 minutes, reducing alert noise by about 60%, improving API response times by 35%, and mentoring engineers through code reviews, pairing, and workshops.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Co-led OpenZeppelin Defender 2.0 Secure Audit as one of two engineers, building a TypeScript and React module that helped smart contract auditors work with clients, track findings, verify fixes, and deliver final PDF reports.
Built Stripe billing integration for Defender 2.0 self-serve Pro subscriptions and enterprise-tier plans, covering payment flows across user-facing screens and backend API work.

Owned the EVM side of the Rust-based OpenZeppelin Relayer OSS work, including chain-specific transaction cost handling, RPC balancing, retry and failover behavior, AWS KMS and GCP KMS signer integrations, and a fix for a critical nonce race condition.
Cut alert noise by about 60% across Slack and PagerDuty by tuning recoverable RPC and gas-spike alerts, making on-call incidents easier to triage.
Led end-to-end testing for Defender with Playwright on critical user flows, improving release confidence for a complex Web3 security product.

Refactored the TypeScript monorepo build system with ESM migration, package exports, bundler and tsconfig updates, and lint/typecheck flow, reducing local and CI build times from about 15 minutes to 3 minutes.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Built a React and TypeScript presentation feature in MyPremise that surfaced up-to-date, often live data through a single shareable URL, reducing friction for account managers and clients sharing reports.
Contributed to the migration from REST to GraphQL with Apollo across key entities, including Express.js service updates, improving API response times by 35% and lowering server usage.
Expanded Jest coverage and added GitHub Actions quality gates, reducing release cycles from one week to less than 24 hours by cutting manual testing effort.
Standardized UI work with Material UI and Storybook, reducing prototyping time from one week to one day while improving consistency across shared components.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Led development of an internal integration and onboarding platform with Go, Node.js, and Angular, saving $75,000 annually and helping customer service teams onboard merchants faster.

Implemented real-time catalog and pricing updates with Go and Kafka, moving core logic from a Python ETL flow so product and discount changes reached the app faster and contributed to a 21% increase in CSAT.
Engineered a Redis-based conflict resolution layer that handled duplicate and out-of-order updates, improving product data accuracy and reliability by 40%.

workExperience.title: System Architect
description: Implemented transaction-related API flows with Express.js, including Transbank payment processing use cases across user-facing flows and backend API work.
Worked with product, engineering, and infrastructure teams to simplify release flow for a legacy internal platform, reducing delivery cycles from months to about two weeks.
Modernized key parts of the stack from ASP.NET to Angular and from .NET Framework to .NET Core, removing Internet Explorer-only constraints and enabling Chrome compatibility.

workExperience.title: Full Stack Developer
description: Delivered more than five web and mobile products for clients across medical, industrial, government, legal, and cultural sectors, working end to end from discovery through implementation.
Built React Native applications for iOS and Android client products, including training apps for Eucerin and a medical clinic plus an employee check-in/check-out app.
Led architecture decisions and set engineering standards for TypeScript, React, React Native, and Redux across the company.
Implemented payment gateway integrations with Express.js services, including Transbank transaction flows for client projects.
Mentored five developers through pairing sessions, code reviews, and workshops focused on architecture, testing, and code quality.

workExperience.title: Fullstack Developer
description: Used WebSockets for real-time courier coordinate transmission, allowing customers to see where their order was coming from.

Built platform capabilities for geolocation, real-time operational features, and product ETL workflows across Python and Go services while helping launch one of Chile’s first Uber Eats-style delivery services.

workExperience.title: Junior Software Developer
description: Started as a junior developer and took on growing frontend ownership, helping introduce Node.js and AngularJS into the agency stack while supporting ecommerce and payment integration flows across client projects.

education.degree: Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science

company_profile.description: Payward is the parent company behind Kraken and a portfolio of products spanning trading, custody, payments, lending, onchain finance, and benchmarks. Kraken is a globally remote crypto and financial infrastructure platform serving retail users, professional traders, institutions, and partner platforms.
company_profile.mission: Accelerate the global adoption of crypto so everyone can achieve financial freedom and inclusion.
company_profile.vision: Build open financial infrastructure that expands access, reduces inefficiencies, and helps financial technologies serve people around the world.
values: High standards for reliable financial infrastructure
company_profile.industry: Crypto, fintech, trading, payments, and financial infrastructure
company_profile.business_model: Financial infrastructure and trading platform with exchange, institutional, API, custody, on/off-ramp, tokenized asset, payments, and partner-embedded services.
recent_signals: Kraken says it is trusted by 13+ million people around the world and has operated for more than 14 years.

Payward states its platform operates across 190+ jurisdictions and has 100+ licenses and registrations spanning crypto assets, securities and derivatives, payments, money transmission, banking, and lending.
Payward Services advertises 15+ years in operation, 190+ countries supported, and $2T+ annual transaction volume.
Kraken careers describes the company as globally remote with the line: Work from anywhere, build the future of finance.
Kraken security pages state that protecting funds and privacy is the company’s number one objective and highlight ISO/IEC 27001:2022 and SOC 2 controls.
